title: Narrative organization
analysis: This task implements narratives.py with two main functions: build_master_storyboard() and propose_narratives(). The codebase already has a solid pattern from classify.py (LLM-backed activity labeling) which we should mirror closely — same approach for loading prompts, calling Anthropic, parsing JSON responses, and error handling. The narrative_planner.md prompt already exists with creator profile placeholders. CatalogDB already has full CRUD for narratives table (insert_narrative, get_narrative, list_narratives, update_narrative_status) and all data access methods we need (get_activity_clusters, get_transcript, get_detections_for_media, get_audio_events_for_media, get_faces_for_media, get_face_clusters). The Narrative dataclass needs to be created with fields matching the LLM response schema (title, activity_cluster_ids, proposed_duration_seconds, arc, emotional_journey, reasoning). build_master_storyboard() will iterate over activity clusters and produce a structured text storyboard, enriching each cluster with: duration from media files, transcript highlights, YOLO event density peaks, face cluster labels, emotional tone from audio events + transcript sentiment, and visual quality notes from detection confidence. propose_narratives() follows the classify.py _call_llm pattern: load narrative_planner.md, fill creator placeholders from config, call Claude Opus (planning_model), parse JSON array response into Narrative objects, and store in DB. A format_for_review() helper will pretty-print proposed narratives for the human checkpoint.

prerequisites.description: Create empty autopilot/organize/narratives.py with module docstring, __all__, logger, NarrativeError exception, and Narrative dataclass stub so imports pass

steps.description: Write failing tests for public API surface: NarrativeError importable and is Exception subclass, Narrative dataclass has required fields (narrative_id, title, description, proposed_duration_seconds, activity_cluster_ids, arc, emotional_journey, reasoning, status), build_master_storyboard and propose_narratives have correct signatures, format_for_review exists

Implement Narrative dataclass with all fields and export in __all__. Add function stubs for build_master_storyboard(db) -> str, propose_narratives(storyboard, db, config) -> list[Narrative], and format_for_review(narratives) -> str. Update organize/__init__.py exports.

Write failing tests for _build_cluster_summary() helper: given a cluster with media, transcripts, detections, audio events, and faces in the DB, returns a dict with keys: label, description, duration, key_moments, people_present, emotional_tone, visual_quality_notes. Test with full data, empty data, and corrupt JSON resilience (mirrors test_classify.py patterns)

Implement _build_cluster_summary(cluster, db) that queries all signals per cluster: computes total duration from media files, extracts transcript highlights, identifies YOLO event density peaks, gathers face cluster labels, infers emotional tone from audio events + transcript content, and computes visual quality notes from detection confidence distributions

Write failing tests for build_master_storyboard(): given activity clusters in DB with labels/descriptions and associated data, produces structured text containing each cluster's summary sections. Test with multiple clusters, empty DB, and clusters without labels

Implement build_master_storyboard(db) that iterates get_activity_clusters(), calls _build_cluster_summary() for each, and formats all summaries into a structured text storyboard suitable for LLM consumption

Write failing tests for _load_and_fill_prompt(config): loads narrative_planner.md, fills all 6 creator profile placeholders from CreatorConfig. Test that all placeholders are replaced, and missing prompt file raises NarrativeError

Implement _load_and_fill_prompt(config) that reads narrative_planner.md and fills {creator_name}, {channel_style}, {target_audience}, {default_video_duration}, {narration_style}, {music_preference} from AutopilotConfig.creator

Write failing tests for _call_llm() / _parse_narratives(): mock Anthropic API call, verify it uses planning_model (not utility_model), sends storyboard as user message, system prompt from narrative_planner.md. Test JSON array parsing into Narrative objects, error handling for API failures, malformed JSON, missing required fields, and empty response

Implement _call_llm(storyboard, config) that calls Claude Opus via Anthropic client with filled system prompt and storyboard user message, and _parse_narratives(text) that extracts JSON array from response and validates/converts each entry into a Narrative object with generated narrative_id

Write failing tests for propose_narratives() end-to-end: with mocked LLM, verify it calls _call_llm with the storyboard text, parses response into Narrative list, stores each narrative in DB via insert_narrative with status='proposed', and returns the list. Test idempotency (re-running doesn't duplicate), empty storyboard handling

Implement propose_narratives(storyboard, db, config) that calls LLM, parses into Narrative objects, and stores each in the narratives table with status='proposed'. Handle errors gracefully with NarrativeError wrapping

Write failing tests for format_for_review(narratives): given a list of Narrative objects, produces human-readable formatted text with numbered narratives showing title, duration, included clusters, arc, emotional journey, and reasoning. Test with single narrative, multiple narratives, and empty list
Implement format_for_review(narratives) -> str that pretty-prints each Narrative with clear headers and sections suitable for creator review at the human checkpoint

Write integration test: full pipeline from seeded DB with media, transcripts, detections, audio events, faces, and activity clusters -> build_master_storyboard -> propose_narratives (mocked LLM) -> format_for_review. Verify end-to-end data flow and DB state
Fix any integration issues, ensure organize/__init__.py re-exports NarrativeError, Narrative, build_master_storyboard, propose_narratives, format_for_review. Final cleanup pass

Write failing tests for build_master_storyboard() corrupt-cluster resilience: (1) test_corrupt_cluster_skipped_gracefully: seed DB with 3 activity clusters where the 2nd has corrupt/invalid clip_ids_json (e.g. 'NOT_JSON'). Call build_master_storyboard() and assert it returns a storyboard containing sections for clusters 1 and 3 but not cluster 2, proving one bad row does not abort the whole build. (2) test_corrupt_cluster_logged: same setup but capture log output (caplog) and assert a WARNING or ERROR is logged that includes the corrupt cluster's cluster_id so operators can investigate.

Fix build_master_storyboard() to wrap the _build_cluster_summary() call inside a try/except NarrativeError block within the cluster iteration loop. In the except branch, log a warning including the cluster_id and the error message, then continue to the next cluster. This ensures one corrupt cluster row does not crash the entire storyboard build.

Write failing tests for propose_narratives() transactional insert and error wrapping: (1) test_partial_insert_rolls_back: mock db.insert_narrative to succeed on first call then raise sqlite3.IntegrityError on second call. Assert that propose_narratives raises NarrativeError (not a raw DB exception), and that NO narratives are persisted in the DB (list_narratives returns empty), proving the transaction was rolled back atomically. (2) test_db_error_wrapped_as_narrative_error: mock db.insert_narrative to raise a generic sqlite3.OperationalError. Assert propose_narratives raises NarrativeError with the original exception chained (__cause__), honouring the function's docstring contract.

Fix propose_narratives() to wrap the entire insert loop in a 'with db.conn:' block for SQLite transaction semantics (auto-commit on success, auto-rollback on exception). Additionally, wrap the insert block in a try/except that catches any Exception from the DB layer (sqlite3.Error and subclasses) and re-raises as NarrativeError with the original exception chained, honouring the function's Raises contract.

design_decisions.decision: Use planning_model (Claude Opus) not utility_model (Claude Sonnet) for narrative proposals
design_decisions.rationale: The task spec says 'Claude Opus narrative proposals' and LLMConfig has planning_model=claude-opus-4 for high-level creative planning vs utility_model for simpler classification tasks

design_decisions.decision: Narrative dataclass includes all LLM response fields plus narrative_id and status
design_decisions.rationale: narrative_id is generated server-side (UUID) before DB insertion. status defaults to 'proposed' matching the DB schema. arc is kept as a dict with beginning/middle/end keys matching the prompt template's output format

design_decisions.decision: build_master_storyboard returns str (not structured data) for LLM consumption
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec says 'Format as structured text for LLM consumption'. The storyboard is an intermediate artifact passed directly to the LLM, so string is the natural type. Internal helper _build_cluster_summary returns dicts for testability

Both need to query the same DB signals (transcripts, detections, audio events) per cluster, but narratives.py enriches with additional signals (duration, faces, visual quality). Keeping as a separate function enables targeted unit testing

design_decisions.decision: format_for_review as a standalone function, not embedded in propose_narratives
design_decisions.rationale: The task spec identifies this as a 'human review checkpoint' with specific formatting needs. Keeping it separate allows re-formatting without re-querying the LLM and makes it independently testable

design_decisions.decision: Fill prompt template placeholders from AutopilotConfig rather than LLMConfig alone
design_decisions.rationale: The narrative_planner.md prompt has {creator_name}, {channel_style} etc. which live in CreatorConfig, a sibling of LLMConfig under AutopilotConfig. The function signature should accept the full config or at least creator + llm sections. We'll pass AutopilotConfig to propose_narratives for access to both creator profile and LLM settings

design_decisions.decision: build_master_storyboard skips corrupt clusters instead of aborting
design_decisions.rationale: Review feedback: _build_cluster_summary raises NarrativeError on corrupt clip_ids_json. A single bad cluster row should not crash the entire storyboard build — this is a real-world data boundary. Log the error with cluster_id and continue to the next cluster. Matches the defensive pattern used elsewhere in the codebase (e.g. corrupt segments_json in _build_cluster_summary itself logs and continues).

design_decisions.decision: propose_narratives uses transactional insert with 'with db.conn:' and wraps DB errors as NarrativeError
design_decisions.rationale: Review feedback: inserting narratives one-by-one without a transaction leaves the DB in a partial state if insert N fails. CatalogDB documents that write methods do NOT auto-commit and callers must use 'with db:' or 'with db.conn:' for atomic operations. cluster.py already uses this pattern (line 310). Additionally, the docstring contract says 'Raises: NarrativeError' but raw DB exceptions were leaking — wrapping them honours the contract.

reuse.how: Mirror the pattern: load prompt -> format user message -> call anthropic.Anthropic().messages.create() -> parse JSON from response (handle ```json blocks) -> validate required fields -> wrap errors in custom exception
Same DB query pattern for transcripts, detections, audio events per clip_ids. Extend with duration computation, face cluster queries, and confidence distribution analysis
Direct use for storing proposed narratives with status='proposed' and reading back for verification

reuse.where: autopilot/db.py get_activity_clusters(), get_transcript(), get_detections_for_media(), get_audio_events_for_media(), get_faces_for_media(), get_face_clusters()
reuse.how: Query all analysis data for storyboard construction - same methods used by classify.py
Already authored with creator profile placeholders and JSON output format. Load and fill placeholders at runtime
In-memory SQLite DB with autocommit for all unit tests - same fixture used by test_classify.py and test_cluster.py
Replicate the MagicMock + patch.dict(sys.modules) approach for mocking anthropic module in tests, adapted for JSON array responses
LLMConfig.planning_model for Opus model selection, CreatorConfig for prompt template filling

cluster.py uses 'with db.conn:' for atomic clear+insert of activity clusters. CatalogDB docstring documents that write methods do NOT auto-commit and callers should use context manager for commit/rollback. Apply same pattern to propose_narratives insert loop.
